label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a passage describing the process of looking for a missing child. The options are A to D. First, I need to understand the passage. The first step is putting an APB (All-Points Bulletin) to all police stations. Then they call hospitals, check if the child was in an accident. Then they contact parents, friends, relatives, follow movements, check local or national papers. Other methods include posters, TV, and a magazine with photos of missing children. But the passage also says that with nearly two million missing children every year, they can't do all these things for everyone because of time, money, and staff constraints. The question is asking what the passage says about when they are looking for a missing child. The options are: A) can always find him – but the passage says they can't do all methods for everyone, so maybe they don't always find them. B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above – the passage says they can't do all these things for everyone because of limitations, so this might be correct. C) just try one way or another – but the passage lists multiple methods, though not all may be used for each case, so maybe not exactly. D) won't give up until they find him – the passage doesn't mention perseverance to that extent, just practical limitations. So the correct answer
